entities.name: CROP INTENSIFICATION
entities.description: The process of increasing crop production per unit area to meet food demands

entities.name: FOOD DEMANDS
entities.description: The requirement for food to sustain an increasing population

entities.name: INCREASING POPULATION
entities.description: The growth in human population driving the need for more food

entities.name: LANDSCAPE-BASED INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Resource management approaches applied across a landscape to improve moisture and productivity

entities.name: INDIVIDUAL PLOT-LEVEL INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Agricultural interventions applied to single plots, focusing on fertilizers and cultivars

entities.name: INDIAN SEMI-ARID TROPICS
entities.description: The geographical region where the scaling-up initiatives were implemented

entities.name: SOIL AND WATER CONSERVATION INTERVENTIONS
entities.description: Decentralized measures implemented to conserve soil and water within a landscape

entities.name: BALANCED FERTILIZER APPLICATION
entities.description: The practice of applying nutrients in correct proportions to improve crop yield

entities.name: IMPROVED CROP CULTIVARS
entities.description: Plant varieties developed for enhanced performance and yield

entities.name: MOISTURE AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of water available in the soil, critical for reducing crop failure and enabling intensification

entities.name: CROP FAILURE
entities.description: The total or partial loss of a crop due to environmental risks

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY GAIN
entities.description: The increase in agricultural output resulting from interventions

entities.name: LAND AND WATER-USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The effectiveness of utilizing land and water resources for crop production

entities.name: SURFACE RUNOFF
entities.description: Water from precipitation that flows over the land surface

entities.name: GROUNDWATER RECHARGE
entities.description: The process of water moving from the surface into underground aquifers

entities.name: RAINFALL ZONES
entities.description: Areas categorized by their annual precipitation levels, specifically 550–800 mm in this study

entities.name: CROP YIELD
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit area

entities.name: CROP PRODUCTION
entities.description: The total annual output of crops

entities.name: INCOME GENERATION
entities.description: The creation of financial earnings for farmers through increased production

entities.name: WATER PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The amount of crop produced per unit of water used

entities.name: SOIL LOSS
entities.description: The removal of the top layer of soil through erosion

entities.name: BASE FLOW AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The portion of streamflow derived from groundwater discharge

entities.name: UNTREATED WATERSHEDS
entities.description: Watershed areas that did not receive landscape-based interventions

entities.name: FALLOW LANDS
entities.description: Land left unseeded that can be utilized for cultivation with increased moisture

entities.name: UNITED NATIONS SUSTAINABLE DEVELOPMENT GOALS
entities.description: Global targets for sustainable development that the study's findings aim to support
